summary: Day 1 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Michael Jordan (Widely attributed / Nike 'Failure' Commercial (1997)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I've failed over and over and over again in my life. And that is why I succeed....' The daily micro-action is a Hydration task: Drink 2 liters of water today.

Day 2 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Michael Jordan (I Can't Accept Not Trying (1994)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I can accept failure. Everyone fails at something. But I can't accept not trying....' The daily micro-action is a Fruit Intake task: Eat 2 pieces of fruit today.

Day 3 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Kobe Bryant (The Mamba Mentality: How I Play (2018)). The message encourages them to think about: 'What I'm doing right now, I'm chasing perfection....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Say 'thank you' to your coach after practice.

Day 4 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Michael Jordan (I Can't Accept Not Trying (1994)). The message encourages them to think about: 'Talent wins games, but teamwork and intelligence wins championships....' The daily micro-action is a Kindness task: High-five every teammate today.

Day 5 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Kobe Bryant (The Mamba Mentality: How I Play (2018)). The message encourages them to think about: 'Be authentic, and let them like you or not for who you actually are....' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Share one thing you're proud of with a parent.

Day 6 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Kobe Bryant (Interview with Patrick Bet-David (2019)). The message encourages them to think about: 'Get over yourself! 'Cause it's not about you, man. You are not that important!...' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Help clean up equipment after your session.

Day 7 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Michael Jordan (How to be like Mike (2005)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I set another goal … a reasonable, manageable goal that I could realistically achieve if I worked ha...' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Write down your goal for this week.

Day 8 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Michael Jordan (Hall of Fame Induction Speech (2009)). The message encourages them to think about: 'Limits, like fears, are often just an illusion....' The daily micro-action is a Resilience task: Try one new skill or drill you find difficult today.

Day 9 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Serena Williams (Widely attributed interview). The message encourages them to think about: 'I really think a champion is defined not by their wins but by how they can recover when they fall....' The daily micro-action is a Hydration task: Drink a glass of water first thing in the morning.

Day 10 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Vince Lombardi (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'The only place success comes before work is in the dictionary....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Pack your sports bag the night before.

Day 11 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Arthur Ashe (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'One important key to success is self-confidence. An important key to self-confidence is preparation....' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Stretch for 10 minutes before bed.

Day 12 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Pat Riley (The Winner Within (1993)). The message encourages them to think about: 'Excellence is the gradual result of always striving to do better....' The daily micro-action is a Fruit Intake task: Eat a healthy snack instead of chips or sweets today.

Day 13 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Muhammad Ali (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'I hated every minute of training, but I said, 'Don't quit. Suffer now and live the rest of your life...' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Do 5 extra push-ups or sit-ups.

Day 14 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Charles Barkley (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'If you are afraid of failure you don't deserve to be successful!...' The daily micro-action is a Kindness task: Cheer for a teammate when they make a mistake.

Day 15 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Tiger Woods (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'No matter how good you get, you can always get better, and that's the exciting part....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Ask your coach for one thing you can improve.

Day 16 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Billie Jean King (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'Champions keep playing until they get it right....' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Practice your 'weak' side (left hand/foot) for 5 minutes.

Day 17 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Mia Hamm (Go For the Goal (1999)). The message encourages them to think about: 'The backbone of success is hard work, determination, good planning, and the perseverance to see it t...' The daily micro-action is a Hydration task: Drink water instead of a sugary drink at lunch.

Day 18 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Roger Federer (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'I'm a very positive thinker, and I think that is what helps me the most in difficult moments....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Hold the door open for someone today.

Day 19 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Jackie Robinson (I Never Had It Made (1972)). The message encourages them to think about: 'A life is not important except in the impact it has on other lives....' The daily micro-action is a Kindness task: Give a compliment to someone at school.

Day 20 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Usain Bolt (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'I don't think limits....' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Focus on beating your own best time or score today.

Day 21 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Ian Thorpe (Widely attributed (Australian Athlete)). The message encourages them to think about: 'For me, losing is not coming second. It’s coming out of a swim and knowing that you could have done ...' The daily micro-action is a Hydration task: Carry a water bottle with you all day.

Day 22 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Don Bradman (Widely attributed (Australian Cricket Legend)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I was never coached; I just played the game....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Shake hands with your opponent after the match.

Day 23 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Cathy Freeman (Widely attributed (Australian Athlete)). The message encourages them to think about: 'You got to believe in yourself....' The daily micro-action is a Fruit Intake task: Have a piece of fruit as your afternoon snack.

Day 24 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Ash Barty (Widely attributed (Australian Athlete)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I’m just a girl from Brisbane who happens to play tennis....' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Reflect on one thing you did well today.

Day 25 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Sam Kerr (Widely attributed (Australian Soccer Star)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I don’t really care what people think of me, as long as I’m happy....' The daily micro-action is a Resilience task: Smile when things get tough in training.

Day 26 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Ellyse Perry (Widely attributed (Australian Dual-Sport Star)). The message encourages them to think about: 'It’s about making the most of every opportunity....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Say 'Please' and 'Thank you' to your parents/guardians today.

Day 27 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Derek Jeter (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'There may be people who have more talent than you, but there’s no excuse for anyone to work harder t...' The daily micro-action is a Kindness task: Help a younger player with their skills.

Day 28 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Simone Biles (Courage to Soar (2016)). The message encourages them to think about: 'I’d rather regret the risks that didn’t work out than the chances I didn’t take at all....' The daily micro-action is a Hydration task: Drink a glass of water before every meal.

Day 29 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Tom Brady (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'I didn’t come this far to only come this far....' The daily micro-action is a Personal Best task: Set a Personal Best in one exercise today.

Day 30 focuses on the theme of resilience with a quote from Muhammad Ali (Widely attributed). The message encourages them to think about: 'It isn't the mountains ahead to climb that wear you out; it's the pebble in your shoe....' The daily micro-action is a Manners task: Clean your sports shoes or equipment today.
